summary:
Vitamin C enhances the absorption of non-heme iron according to multiple studies; no clear benefit has been shown from adding Thiamine or Riboflavin directly into an iron-absorbing regimen based on current research findings.
To improve conciseness further:

Vitamin C boosts non-heme iron absorption per study results; existing data shows little impact from using Thiamine or Riboflavin alone for increasing iron intake efficiency.
The summary now adheres strictly to two concise sentences focusing only on critical information pertinent to identifying which vitamin aids in absorbing iron effectively among given options. The response highlights specific outcomes related to each mentioned nutrient’s effect on iron metabolism accurately reflecting Agent2's analysis but condensing it efficiently under strict length constraints provided by your request format guidelines.
